channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Senate Republicans Consider Restrictions on Trump’s Anti-Weaponization Fund
item.description: Senate Republicans are considering measures to impose restrictions on how former President Donald Trump can distribute funds from his $1.776 billion 'Anti-Weaponization Fund.' The fund, established as...

item.title: Congress MLAs Rajesh Kumar and P Viswanathan to join Tamil Nadu cabinet after 59 years, Rajya Sabha discussions underway
item.description: In a historic political development, the Congress party is joining the Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay-led Tamil Nadu government on Thursday, marking its first cabinet participation in the state after 5...

item.title: Alberta committee meeting on referendum disrupted by premature UCP statement
item.description: Chaos unfolded at a special committee meeting of the Alberta legislature on Wednesday, which was reviewing Thomas Lukaszuk’s Forever Canadian petition. The committee was tasked with deciding whether t...

item.title: Understanding How Retirement Village Fees Work in the ACT
item.description: Moving into a retirement village in the ACT is a significant financial decision that differs markedly from purchasing standard residential property. The key financial components include the entry cont...

item.title: Comelec plans to announce all BARMM parliamentary election winners within two days
item.description: The Commission on Elections (Comelec) announced that it aims to proclaim all winners of the first Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BARMM) parliamentary elections within two days after ...

item.title: The Significance of Standing Ovations at the Cannes Film Festival
item.description: Standing ovations have long been a defining feature of the Cannes Film Festival, which this year runs from May 12 to 23. According to Barry Hertz, The Globe's film editor, the length and intensity of ...

item.title: Sewage Overflow Disrupts Durban Hotel Operations and Leads to Beach Closures
item.description: A technical failure at the Mahatma Gandhi Pump Station near Durban's Point area has caused significant disruptions for local businesses and public spaces. Over the past week, sewer water has overflowe...

item.title: High Court Rules in Favour of Audrey Mbugua on Gender Marker Changes
item.description: After more than ten years of struggling with State bureaucracy, humiliating interrogations, and inconsistent official documents, activist Audrey Mbugua Ithibu has achieved a major legal victory. The H...

item.title: President Macron Addresses Challenges in France-Africa Relations at Africa Forward Summit
item.description: The Africa Forward Summit, co-chaired by French President Emmanuel Macron and Kenyan President William Ruto, convened a wide array of African leaders alongside UN Secretary-General António Guterres, a...

item.title: Femi Otedola to Invest Geregu Sale Proceeds into Dangote Refinery IPO
item.description: Femi Otedola, wey be chairman of First HoldCo, don talk say the money wey e make from selling him stake for Geregu Power Plc go dey used for the planned private placement and IPO of Dangote Petroleum ...

item.title: Edmonton announces 2026 outdoor pool openings and new policies
item.description: Edmonton’s outdoor pools are officially opening for the summer 2026 season, with the city implementing some changes this year. Residents can now make online reservations for any of the five outdoor po...

item.title: Gilbert Rozon Agrees to $930,000 Settlement with Nine Women Over Sexual Assault Cases
item.description: Gilbert Rozon, the founder of the Just for Laughs comedy festival, has agreed to pay $930,000 to nine women who accused him of sexual misconduct. The settlement follows a ruling by Quebec Superior Cou...

item.title: James Dean’s Teenage Years in 1940s Indiana
item.description: Before becoming the iconic 'Rebel Without a Cause,' James Dean was a typical teenager growing up in Fairmount, Indiana, during the 1940s. At Fairmount High School, he excelled in sports, particularly ...

item.title: Daniel Ray Leonard arrested near his father’s Los Angeles home for breaching restraining order
item.description: Daniel Ray Leonard, the 25-year-old son of legendary American boxer Sugar Ray Leonard, was apprehended by police at his father's Los Angeles residence after allegedly attempting to enter the property ...

item.title: Jet2 Expands Summer 2026 Schedule with 37 New Routes and 3 New Destinations
item.description: Jet2 has announced a significant expansion of its summer 2026 operations, introducing 37 new routes and three new destinations—La Palma, Palermo, and Samos—bringing the airline's total overseas destin...

item.title: Universities increasingly rescind commencement invitations over controversial views
item.description: In recent years, universities across the United States have increasingly rescinded invitations to commencement speakers due to student protests over controversial viewpoints. Former Northwestern Unive...

item.title: Supreme Court permits care of stray dogs with legal responsibility for bites
item.description: In a significant ruling, the Supreme Court of India has allowed individuals and organisations to care for and feed stray dogs in public spaces, provided they accept legal responsibility for any injuri...

item.title: Philippine Navy Plans Fleet Modernization with Warships and Anti-Sub Helicopters by 2028
item.description: The Philippine Navy announced plans to modernize its fleet with the acquisition of four new warships and six anti-submarine warfare (ASW) helicopters in the coming years, as part of the Armed Forces o...

item.title: Winnipeg Police Seize $37M in Drugs, Arrest 33 in Largest Manitoba Bust
item.description: Winnipeg police have made what they are calling the largest drug bust in Manitoba's history, arresting 33 individuals and seizing more than $37.2 million worth of illegal substances including cocaine,...

item.title: Wes Streeting Delivers Unremarkable Resignation Speech in Commons
item.description: In his first Commons speech since resigning from the cabinet, Wes Streeting delivered a 19-minute address that offered little substance. Observers noted that the speech was largely composed of platitu...
